title: solanum cultum
parts: Solanum cultum has many medicinal parts, such as the roots, leaves, and berries, which contain various bioactive compounds. The roots of Solanum cultum contain solasodine and solasonine, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various conditions. The leaves of the plant are rich in solanine and solamargine, compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The berries of Solanum cultum are a rich source of glycoalkaloids and flavonoids, which are used to treat various health issues, including fever and pain.
side_effects: Solanum cultum can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal problems, nausea, and vomiting due to its high solanine content. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can also lead to headaches, fatigue, and muscle weakness. In severe cases, improper use of Solanum cultum can result in more serious conditions, including kidney and liver damage, as well as respiratory problems. It is essential to handle and use the plant with caution and under the guidance of a medical professional to avoid these adverse health effects.
